title: Noccaea haussknechtii
taxonomy: Noccaea haussknechtii is classified within the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Noccaea and is a species of flowering plant. More specifically, Noccaea haussknechtii is classified as a member of the subfamily Brassicoideae, which is a part of the Brassicaceae family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants. Noccaea haussknechtii is a species of perennial plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia.

intro: Noccaea haussknechtii, also known as Haussknecht's penny-cress, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Noccaea haussknechtii include isothiocyanates, glucosinolates, and other sulfur-containing compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Haussknecht's penny-cress include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
